mission.title: Microcks: Fix MongoDB Pod Stuck in Pending (PVC Not Bound)
mission.description: Resolves the common issue where the microcks-mongodb pod stays in Pending state because the PersistentVolumeClaim cannot be bound. This happens on clusters without a default StorageClass or on local development clusters (minikube, kind, k3d) without a CSI driver.

steps.title: Identify the pending pod and PVC
steps.description:
Check the MongoDB pod status and its PVC:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n microcks -l app=mongodb
kubectl get pvc -n microcks
kubectl describe pvc -n microcks
```
Look for events like `no persistent volumes available for this claim` or `waiting for a volume to be created`.

steps.title: Check available StorageClasses
steps.description:
Verify your cluster has a default StorageClass:
```bash
kubectl get storageclass
```
A default StorageClass is marked with `(default)` in the output. If none exists, MongoDB cannot provision its PersistentVolume.

steps.title: Option A: Disable persistent storage (development)
steps.description:
For development or testing, disable MongoDB persistence entirely:
```bash
helm upgrade microcks microcks/microcks \
  --namespace microcks \
  --set mongodb.persistent=false \
  --reuse-values
```
This uses emptyDir storage — data is lost when the pod restarts. Suitable for development only.

steps.title: Option B: Set a specific StorageClass (production)
steps.description:
If your cluster has a StorageClass but it is not the default, specify it explicitly:
```bash
# List available classes
kubectl get storageclass

# Upgrade with the specific class
helm upgrade microcks microcks/microcks \
  --namespace microcks \
  --set mongodb.persistent=true \
  --set mongodb.storageClassName=<your-storage-class> \
  --reuse-values
```
Replace `<your-storage-class>` with the name from `kubectl get storageclass`.

steps.title: Verify MongoDB is running
steps.description:
Wait for the MongoDB pod to start:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=mongodb -n microcks --timeout=120s
kubectl get pods -n microcks
```
All pods should be in `Running` state.

resolution.summary: MongoDB PVC pending is resolved by either disabling persistence (development) or specifying an available StorageClass (production). The Microcks Helm chart defaults to persistent=true which requires a default StorageClass.
codeSnippets: helm upgrade microcks microcks/microcks --namespace microcks --set mongodb.persistent=false --reuse-values
